QF.id: XIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_34

question.text: A USDINR futures trader buys 20 lots at 83.25 and the settlement price is 83.45. The lot size is 1000 USD. What is the daily MTM credit?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** MTM = (Settlement - Entry) × Lot size × Lots = (83.45 - 83.25) × 1000 × 20 = 0.20 × 20,000 = Rs 4,000 credit. A 20 paise favorable move on 20 lots of 1000 USD each. **The Trap:** Rs 400 uses only 2 lots. Rs 40,000 adds an extra zero. Rs 2,000 uses 10 lots. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I covers USDINR futures P&L calculations. Currency futures P&L is straightforward: price change × lot size × number of lots. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** 20 paise × 20 lots = Rs 4,000 profit on what might be Rs 30,000-40,000 initial margin — about 10-13% return in one day. This illustrates both the opportunity and risk of leveraged currency trading.
